records_captured: base stat rows for every entry in PokéAPI's /pokemon list, including Mega/regional/alternate forms
type(s), an official-artwork image URL, and height/weight for every entry, read off the same /pokemon response already fetched for base stats

fields.name: pokemon_id
fields.description: National Dex number from the form's PokéAPI species reference; shared across all forms of one species (not the form's own PokéAPI resource id, which is a non-dex internal id for Mega/regional/alternate forms)

fields.name: pokemon_name
fields.description: Species name as reported by PokéAPI

fields.name: form_name
fields.description: The form's own PokéAPI slug; equals the species name for the default/base form

fields.name: stat_total
fields.description: Sum of the six base stats

fields.name: type_2
fields.description: Secondary elemental type (PokéAPI types slot 2); null for single-type Pokémon

fields.name: image_url
fields.description: Official-artwork sprite URL (sprites.other.official-artwork.front_default); null for some alt forms PokéAPI doesn't have official artwork for

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: API endpoint the record was extracted from

fields.name: dataset_version
fields.description: Dataset version this staging snapshot feeds

known_risks: Form-name mismatches between canonical and format-specific sources: this source's form_name follows PokéAPI's own slug convention, which other sources' form labels don't always match verbatim (e.g. OP.GG's "mega-charizard-x" vs. this source's "charizard-mega-x")
